title: Verify project prerequisites and access
description: Verify that all information needed to safely begin implementation is available before feature work starts. Unlike a typical SaaS project, SystemBook has no external paid services, no remote database, and no MCP dependency for the MVP — verification is scoped to local environment variables and the bootstrap admin flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Real secret values are not written to the PRD, task specs, repository, logs, or chat
The user has manually filled `SESSION_SECRET`, `ARGON2_SECRET`, `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`, `INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` in `.env.local` with real local values
No external database connectivity check is required — SQLite file at `DATABASE_PATH` is created locally by the first migration
No MCP servers/tools are required for this project; confirmed no gap exists
No third-party paid service accounts are required for the MVP; confirmed no gap exists
The bootstrap admin flow (seed script reading `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD`) is understood as the only required test user for initial access

steps.description: Read prerequisite requirements from the PRD
steps.details: Open `PROJECT_ROOT/.prd/PRD.md`, section 8 ("Prerequisitos e Acesso"). Confirm there are no external database, MCP, or paid service dependencies for the MVP.

Confirm `PROJECT_ROOT/.env.local` exists and contains `PORT`, `DATABASE_PATH`, `SESSION_SECRET`, `ARGON2_SECRET`, `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`, `INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD`. `PORT` and `DATABASE_PATH` already have sensible non-secret defaults; the remaining four are placeholders (`TODO_FILL_MANUALLY`).

steps.description: Confirm local secret values are filled manually
steps.details: Ask the user to confirm they generated and filled `SESSION_SECRET` and `ARGON2_SECRET` (e.g. via `openssl rand -base64 32`) and chose real `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` values. Never print, store, commit, or copy these values into generated files or chat.

steps.description: Confirm no external database or service access is needed
steps.details: SQLite is a local file created by migrations at container boot (see TASK-4). There is no remote connectivity to verify. Record this explicitly rather than attempting a network check.

steps.description: Confirm no MCPs or paid third-party docs are required
steps.details: This project depends only on public, free documentation (Drizzle, tRPC, Tiptap, Vite, Docker, SQLite FTS5). No MCP server integration is needed for the MVP. Record this as a confirmed non-gap.

steps.description: Confirm bootstrap admin path is understood
steps.details: The only required 'test user' is the admin created by the seed script from `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` on first boot (implemented in TASK-4/TASK-9 flow). Confirm this is documented and no invite/SMTP flow is expected in the MVP.

technicalNotes: This task gates implementation. Do not start dependent feature tasks until prerequisites are verified.
Secrets must stay local in `.env.local` and must never be committed or copied into PRD/task artifacts.
Unlike typical SaaS PRDs, there is no remote DB/MCP gap to negotiate here — the MVP is intentionally zero-external-dependency by design (see PRD section 1, 'Princípios norteadores').
